ABILITYINFO.name: Not a Wizard
ABILITYINFO.text: Despite the name or appearance, this character is NOT a spellcaster — he knows no spells and never makes casting rolls.

ABILITYINFO.name: Immune to Fear
ABILITYINFO.text: Never has to take Fear tests. Note: this does NOT mean the model causes fear.

ABILITYINFO.name: Leader
ABILITYINFO.text: Friendly models within 6" may use the leader's (usually better) Leadership value for their Leadership tests.

ABILITYINFO.name: Fearless
ABILITYINFO.text: Immune to fear, terror and all-alone tests.

ABILITYINFO.name: Fear
ABILITYINFO.text: Causes fear. A model that wants to charge a fear-causing model must first pass a Leadership test; in close combat against one it only hits if a Leadership test is passed (otherwise only on a 6). Fear-causers are themselves immune to fear.

ABILITYINFO.name: Terror
ABILITYINFO.text: Like fear, but stronger: nearby models can be forced to flee, and charging a terror-causer requires a passed Leadership test.

ABILITYINFO.name: Large Target
ABILITYINFO.text: May always be chosen as a shooting target, even if it is not the nearest. In addition, all shots against it gain +1 to hit.

ABILITYINFO.name: Animal
ABILITYINFO.text: Subject only to animal-relevant rules; gains no experience and cannot become a hero.

ABILITYINFO.name: Immune to Psychology
ABILITYINFO.text: Ignores all psychology rules (fear, terror, frenzy, hatred, stupidity, all-alone tests).

ABILITYINFO.name: Immune to Poison
ABILITYINFO.text: Is not affected by poison or poisoned weapons.

ABILITYINFO.name: No Pain
ABILITYINFO.text: Treats a "stunned" result as "knocked down" — these warriors feel no pain.

ABILITYINFO.name: No Experience
ABILITYINFO.text: This model gains no experience and never advances.

ABILITYINFO.__re: darf nicht rennen|may not run|nicht rennen

ABILITYINFO.name: May Not Run
ABILITYINFO.text: Can never run; moves at most its normal movement distance.

ABILITYINFO.name: Frenzy
ABILITYINFO.text: Must charge if an enemy is within reach; doubles its attacks in close combat; immune to fear and terror. Ends as soon as the model is knocked down or stunned.

ABILITYINFO.name: Hatred
ABILITYINFO.text: May re-roll failed to-hit rolls against the hated enemy in the first close-combat round.

ABILITYINFO.name: Stupidity
ABILITYINFO.text: Leadership test at the start of the turn. Failed: the model staggers aimlessly forward or stands idle and cannot charge, shoot or cast.

ABILITYINFO.name: Wizard
ABILITYINFO.text: Can cast spells or prayers — see the warband's magic/prayers panel.

ABILITYINFO.name: Charge
ABILITYINFO.text: Gains extra attack(s) in the turn it charges — details in the unit text.

ABILITYINFO.name: Unarmed
ABILITYINFO.text: Fights without weapons at no penalty (does not grant the enemy the usual bonus against unarmed opponents).

ABILITYINFO.name: All Alone
ABILITYINFO.text: If a model fights without friendly models within 6" against several opponents, it must pass a Leadership test — otherwise it flees.

ABILITYINFO.name: Cold Blooded
ABILITYINFO.text: For psychology and rout tests roll 3D6 and take the two lowest.

ABILITYINFO.name: Scaly Skin
ABILITYINFO.text: Natural armour save from thick scales (not modifiable beyond 6; "no save" cancels it).

ABILITYINFO.name: Regeneration
ABILITYINFO.text: May recover lost wounds in the recovery phase (roll depends on the unit).

ABILITYINFO.name: Blood Greed
ABILITYINFO.text: Tends to frenzy as enemies fall — details in the unit text.

ABILITYINFO.name: Sprint
ABILITYINFO.text: May run three times instead of twice its movement distance.

ABILITYINFO.name: Flammable
ABILITYINFO.text: Fire attacks cause double the number of wounds.

ABILITYINFO.name: Bite Attack
ABILITYINFO.text: Extra close-combat attack with the teeth (strikes last, uses the model's own Strength).

ABILITYINFO.name: Mutation
ABILITYINFO.text: May buy a mutation — see the warband's mutation list.

ABILITYINFO.name: Infiltration
ABILITYINFO.text: Is set up last, anywhere out of sight of the enemy (mind the minimum distance).

ABILITYINFO.name: Natural Stealth
ABILITYINFO.text: A master of moving unseen: while the model is Hidden, enemies trying to detect it halve their Initiative (i.e. it is far harder to spot). (Dark Elf Shade)

ABILITYINFO.name: Scout
ABILITYINFO.text: May be deployed differently or helps in the exploration phase — details in the unit text.

ABILITYINFO.name: Animosity
ABILITYINFO.text: At the start of your turn roll a D6 for each Orc/Goblin Henchman not already in close combat. On a 1, roll again: 1 = “I ’erd dat!” charges/shoots the nearest friendly Orc or Goblin; 2–5 = “Wud yoo say?” hurls insults and does nothing else; 6 = “I’ll show yer!” makes an extra move toward the nearest enemy, charging if able.

ABILITYINFO.name: Hatred
ABILITYINFO.text: Hatred: in the first turn of any close combat against a hated enemy, may re-roll all missed to-hit rolls. (Cannot leave combat with a hated foe while able to fight.)

ABILITYINFO.name: Aquatic
ABILITYINFO.text: Moves through water with no movement penalty and counts as being in cover while in or behind water.

ABILITYINFO.name: Berserk
ABILITYINFO.text: Subject to Frenzy: must charge any enemy in reach, doubles its Attacks in close combat, and is immune to fear and being All Alone (see Frenzy).

ABILITYINFO.name: Bestial
ABILITYINFO.text: An animal mind: immune to all psychology and never takes All Alone tests, but can never be the warband leader.

ABILITYINFO.name: Prayers of Sigmar
ABILITYINFO.text: A devout servant of Sigmar: each turn may attempt one Prayer of Sigmar instead of casting a spell. May not use any other form of magic.

ABILITYINFO.name: Prayers of Taal
ABILITYINFO.text: Each turn may call upon one Prayer of Taal instead of casting a spell.

ABILITYINFO.name: Hang the Bandit
ABILITYINFO.text: Hates Bandits and Outlaws: re-roll missed to-hit rolls in the first round of any close combat against them.

ABILITYINFO.name: Keen Eyesight
ABILITYINFO.text: Spots Hidden enemies at up to twice the model's Initiative in inches.

ABILITYINFO.name: Inspired Cooking
ABILITYINFO.text: Friendly Halflings within 6" of the Master Chef may re-roll failed to-hit rolls in close combat (once per turn).

ABILITYINFO.name: Pit Fighter
ABILITYINFO.text: +1 WS and +1 Attack while fighting in ruins, buildings or The Pit.

ABILITYINFO.name: Evade
ABILITYINFO.text: Agile dodger: when an enemy charges this model it may try to evade — take an Initiative test; if passed, it counts as a failed charge.

ABILITYINFO.name: Grudgebearers
ABILITYINFO.text: Dwarfs bear an ancient grudge against Elves: if the warband ever hires any Elven Hired Sword, this Dwarf leaves the warband immediately.

ABILITYINFO.name: Hard Head
ABILITYINFO.text: Ignores the special rules of maces, clubs and similar concussion weapons — not easily knocked out.

ABILITYINFO.name: Hard to Kill
ABILITYINFO.text: Tough and resilient: only taken Out of Action on a 6 (instead of 5–6); treat 1–2 as knocked down and 3–5 as stunned.

ABILITYINFO.name: Skinny
ABILITYINFO.text: Cowardly and frail: on a Serious Injury roll of 1-3 the Hobgoblin abandons the warband.

ABILITYINFO.name: Nobody cares about them
ABILITYINFO.text: Hobgoblins count as only half a model when working out how many are down for a Rout test. Hobgoblins can never be promoted to Hero (re-roll "The Lad's Got Talent").

ABILITYINFO.name: Nasty Reputation
ABILITYINFO.text: Gaolers are notorious for their brutality — the dread of being captured by them causes fear in Humans.

ABILITYINFO.name: Drudgery
ABILITYINFO.text: Their will is broken: Informers may never become Heroes. Re-roll any "The Lad's Got Talent" result for them.

ABILITYINFO.name: Art of Silent Death
ABILITYINFO.text: Master of open-hand fighting: when fighting unarmed, scores a critical hit on a to-wound roll of 5-6 instead of 6. With a quarter staff, only the unarmed attacks crit on 5+.

ABILITYINFO.name: Ride Horse
ABILITYINFO.text: Trained to ride Horses — may be mounted, using the mounted-model rules.

ABILITYINFO.name: Decree
ABILITYINFO.text: If the Emissary dies, a new one must be hired before any other warrior or equipment is bought; he then reclaims leadership of the warband.

ABILITYINFO.name: Mob
ABILITYINFO.text: +1 Leadership for each other friendly Peasant within 3". Due to their rage, Peasants do not benefit from the Leader rule.

ABILITYINFO.name: Ignored
ABILITYINFO.text: Peasants taken out of action are not counted toward the number of down models for Rout tests.

ABILITYINFO.name: Downtrodden
ABILITYINFO.text: When a Peasant is wounded, do not roll for injury — the model is immediately taken out of action.

ABILITYINFO.name: Simple folk
ABILITYINFO.text: Peasants never gain Experience.

ABILITYINFO.name: Death Wish
ABILITYINFO.text: Seeks an honourable death in combat: completely immune to all psychology.

ABILITYINFO.name: Slow Witted
ABILITYINFO.text: Not the brightest: gains advances at half the normal rate (needs twice the usual experience for each advance).

ABILITYINFO.name: Inconsistency
ABILITYINFO.text: The Condemned's body mutates constantly: its variable characteristics (WS, S, T and A) are re-rolled anew each turn until the player chooses to 'set' a value on an advance.

ABILITYINFO.name: Fate
ABILITYINFO.text: While any characteristic is still variable the Condemned carries no equipment but fights without penalty. Once all are set he equips normally — but if he reaches 90 Experience with a value still variable, he is lost and becomes a Chaos Spawn.

ABILITYINFO.name: Bear Handler
ABILITYINFO.text: A warband with a Bear Tamer may recruit a Trained Bear. The bear automatically passes Stupidity tests while within 6" of the Tamer (even if the Tamer is Knocked Down or Stunned) and may not be fielded in a game the Tamer sits out.

ABILITYINFO.name: Fiercely Loyal
ABILITYINFO.text: The bear's devotion is absolute: it automatically passes Stupidity tests while within 6" of its Bear Tamer — even if the Tamer has been Knocked Down or Stunned.

ABILITYINFO.name: Bear Hug
ABILITYINFO.text: If the bear hits the same enemy with both attacks in a round, it may make a single Bear Hug instead: both roll D6 + Strength; if the bear's total is equal or higher the enemy suffers one automatic wound with no armour save; if lower, the enemy breaks free unharmed.

ABILITYINFO.name: Heirloom (Inheritance)
ABILITYINFO.text: At warband creation the Captain buys one item from the Kislevite Warrior list at half price — a family heirloom. If it is ever lost (e.g. Robbed), he must rebuy the same item at 150% price; until replaced he suffers −1 to all tests and to-hit rolls.

ABILITYINFO.name: Gun-Rest
ABILITYINFO.text: A Streltsi armed with both a berdiche (halberd) and a handgun may brace the handgun on the halberd as a gun-rest, steadying the shot.

ABILITYINFO.name: Crack Shot
ABILITYINFO.text: An expert pistol shot at close quarters: may turn a miss into a hit — re-roll a missed to-hit roll when shooting with a pistol.

ABILITYINFO.name: Quick Reload
ABILITYINFO.text: Drilled to reload black-powder weapons at great speed under pressure — effectively may shoot every turn (no separate reload turn).

ABILITYINFO.name: Expert Weaponsmith
ABILITYINFO.text: While an Instructor is in the warband, all pistols gain +3" range and all other black-powder weapons gain +6" range.

ABILITYINFO.name: Burn the Witch!
ABILITYINFO.text: The Witch Hunter Captain hates all models that can cast spells (wizards) — apply the rules for Hatred against them.

ABILITYINFO.name: Fanatical
ABILITYINFO.text: Convinced the end of the world is nigh: automatically passes every Leadership-based test it is required to take. A Flagellant may never become the warband leader.

ABILITYINFO.name: Expert Swordsman
ABILITYINFO.text: May re-roll missed to-hit rolls in close combat while armed with a sword (FAQ: only normal swords or weeping blades — not double-handed swords or any other weapon).

ABILITYINFO.name: Crazed
ABILITYINFO.text: Driven mad: automatically passes all Leadership-based tests it is required to take.

ABILITYINFO.name: Perfect Killer
ABILITYINFO.text: Strikes with deadly precision: enemies suffer an additional −1 to their armour save against this model's attacks (shooting and close combat).

ABILITYINFO.name: Blessed Sight
ABILITYINFO.text: The blind Augur has second sight: she may re-roll any failed characteristic test and any to-hit roll (close combat or shooting), but must accept the second result. If she is not taken Out of Action, in the exploration phase you may roll two dice for her and keep either.

ABILITYINFO.name: Drunk
ABILITYINFO.text: Meaning depends on the warrior: Ostlander Ruffians (and similar drunken zealots) automatically pass all Leadership-based tests, may never be the leader and carry no missile weapons. A Beastman Centigor instead must test each turn — on its own roll it is subject to Stupidity or Frenzy.

ABILITYINFO.name: Daemonic Aura 5+
ABILITYINFO.text: A protective daemonic field grants a 5+ special save against any wound (not modified by the attack's Strength).

ABILITYINFO.name: Daemon
ABILITYINFO.text: A daemonic creature: causes fear, is immune to psychology and to poison, and gains no experience.

ABILITYINFO.name: Master Weaponsmith
ABILITYINFO.text: While the Engineer is in the warband, the range of the warband's basic missile/black-powder weapons is increased (+3" / +6").

ABILITYINFO.name: Set Traps
ABILITYINFO.text: May lay a hidden trap; a model that triggers it suffers a Strength 4 hit.

ABILITYINFO.name: Weak
ABILITYINFO.text: Too slight for heavy exertion: may not choose advances from the Strength skill list.

ABILITYINFO.name: Vomit Attack
ABILITYINFO.text: Instead of attacking normally, the Troll may vomit over one enemy in base contact: a Strength 5 hit with no armour save allowed.

ABILITYINFO.name: Always Hungry
ABILITYINFO.text: The ever-ravenous Troll must be fed: pay 15 gc upkeep after each game or it wanders off in search of food.

ABILITYINFO.name: Woodland-dweller
ABILITYINFO.text: At home among the trees: ignores movement penalties for woods and rough terrain.

ABILITYINFO.name: Trample
ABILITYINFO.text: +1 Attack in close combat; this additional attack gains no weapon bonuses.

ABILITYINFO.name: Lowest of the low
ABILITYINFO.text: Downtrodden brutes: can never become a Hero (never benefits from "The Lad's Got Talent").

ABILITYINFO.name: Hunter
ABILITYINFO.text: May fire each turn with a handgun or a Hochland long rifle (no separate reload turn needed).

ABILITYINFO.name: Undead
ABILITYINFO.text: Undead: immune to all psychology, never runs, feels no pain, and gains no experience.

ABILITYINFO.name: Sneaky
ABILITYINFO.text: Small and furtive: −1 to hit when shot at (cumulative with other to-hit modifiers).

ABILITYINFO.name: Stealthy
ABILITYINFO.text: Deploys at double the normal scouting distance and begins the game already Hidden.

ABILITYINFO.name: One with the Jungle
ABILITYINFO.text: Suffers no movement penalty for moving through jungle or forest terrain.

ABILITYINFO.name: Black Orc save (6+)
ABILITYINFO.text: A thick, leathery hide grants a 6+ armour save that combines with any worn armour (improving the save by one).

ABILITYINFO.name: Knight's Virtue
ABILITYINFO.text: Chivalrous resolve: never panics or breaks from close combat, and never takes a Leadership test for being All Alone.

ABILITYINFO.name: Life of Slavery
ABILITYINFO.text: A slave may gain experience, but if he rolls "The Lad's Got Talent" the Sheikh executes him (remove from roster) and the henchman group re-rolls the advance.

ABILITYINFO.name: Crazy & Savage
ABILITYINFO.text: Wild, unstable berserkers: +1 Attack in close combat.

ABILITYINFO.name: Rides Spiders
ABILITYINFO.text: Mounted on a Giant or Gigantic Spider, gaining the spider's movement and special rules.

ABILITYINFO.name: Inscribe Runes
ABILITYINFO.text: The Runesmith may inscribe magic runes onto the warband's weapons and armour (starts able to inscribe one minor rune); see the rune rules.

ABILITYINFO.name: Three Throws
ABILITYINFO.text: May make up to three thrown attacks in a single Shooting phase.

ABILITYINFO.name: Concealment
ABILITYINFO.text: Hard to spot: an enemy that wishes to charge a Footpad he cannot see (and who is not Hidden) must subtract 1 from his Initiative before taking the test to detect him.

ABILITYINFO.name: Convincing Manner
ABILITYINFO.text: When an enemy tries to charge any member of the Huckster's warband within 12" of the Huckster, that enemy must first pass a Leadership test; on a failure it may not charge (treat as a failed charge). Creatures subject to Stupidity and animals are immune.

ABILITYINFO.name: Slick Operator
ABILITYINFO.text: Between games the Huckster may run a con: roll a D6 — on 2–6 the warband gains 2D6 gold crowns; on a 1 the con backfires (the Huckster misses the next game and no gold is earned).

ABILITYINFO.name: Looting the Dead
ABILITYINFO.text: For each enemy taken Out of Action, the Looter may roll a D6; on a 4+ he strips all that warrior's equipment, which the warband keeps.

ABILITYINFO.name: Trailblazers
ABILITYINFO.text: Poachers know where to find game and safe camps: in the exploration phase the warband may re-roll one exploration die for each Poacher who took part.

ABILITYINFO.name: Utterly Inept
ABILITYINFO.text: Hopeless rabble: gains no experience and never improves.

ABILITYINFO.name: Hardened
ABILITYINFO.text: Cold-blooded and pitiless: immune to fear and never takes a Leadership test for being All Alone.

ABILITYINFO.name: Swordmaster
ABILITYINFO.text: A master parrier: the Duelist parries when his parry roll equals OR beats the enemy's to-hit roll (not only when it beats it). He must be armed with a weapon that allows a Parry (e.g. a sword or buckler).

ABILITYINFO.name: Weapon Knowledge
ABILITYINFO.text: Versed in every weapon: the Duelist may use any close combat or missile weapon — his starting equipment list merely shows what he begins with.

ABILITYINFO.name: Conch Shell Horn
ABILITYINFO.text: At the start of the game a Piranha Warrior with the horn may re-roll the roll that decides who deploys first and who takes the first turn. Multiple horns cannot force a second re-roll.

ABILITYINFO.name: Combat Riding
ABILITYINFO.text: Trained to fight from horseback: the rider keeps his mount's advantages in close combat (per the Blazing Saddles mounted-combat rules).

ABILITYINFO.name: Horse Handling
ABILITYINFO.text: The Groom has the Animal Handling skill — he keeps his steed under control (e.g. stopping it from bolting), per the Blazing Saddles rules.

ABILITYINFO.name: Ride
ABILITYINFO.text: Allows the warrior to ride a Riding Horse or Warhorse and gain the benefits of a mounted warrior (Blazing Saddles / Mordheim mounted rules). A mounted warrior may not fight with two weapons, though a shield or buckler may still be used.

ABILITYINFO.name: Hardy
ABILITYINFO.text: Self-reliant and used to harsh odds: Heroes and Henchmen may re-roll a failed Leadership test for being All Alone or for Routing — but must accept the second result.

ABILITYINFO.name: Stubborn
ABILITYINFO.text: Set in their ways and impossible to drive off: a Dwarf Longbeard may re-roll any failed Leadership test he is required to take (the second result always stands).

ABILITYINFO.name: Chivalry
ABILITYINFO.text: Knights may never use any missile weapon (except Holy Water), nor any drug or poison, and may never learn spells (prayers are allowed).

ABILITYINFO.name: Lord's Boon
ABILITYINFO.text: On purchase a Knight may start with ONE of a Warhorse, Light Armour or Heavy Armour at half cost. It may never be traded, given away or sold; if the Knight dies it is removed from the warband.

ABILITYINFO.name: Vain
ABILITYINFO.text: Too proud to hide his face from the ladies: may never wear a helmet.

ABILITYINFO.name: Impetuous
ABILITYINFO.text: If not in combat and it did not charge this turn, the Knight Errant must charge a viable standing enemy within range (auto-passing fear/psychology for that charge). You may instead pass a Leadership test to let it act normally.

ABILITYINFO.name: Knighthood
ABILITYINFO.text: On "The Lad's Got Talent" you may keep him a Squire (pick two of Combat/Academic/Strength/Speed) or promote him to a Knight Errant (gains Knight/Vain/Impetuous and the Knight equipment list; may exceed the Knight-Errant limit).

ABILITYINFO.name: Low Caste
ABILITYINFO.text: Gains Experience and advances normally, but re-rolls any "The Lad's Got Talent" result and may never become a Hero.

ABILITYINFO.name: Agony and Ecstasy
ABILITYINFO.text: May spend one Hedonist Whip attack to scourge a friendly model within 3" (auto-hit, +1 to wound); in reward he doubles his Toughness until he fails an Ld test at the start of one of his turns.

ABILITYINFO.name: Pain and Pleasure
ABILITYINFO.text: Whenever the Whipmaster suffers an unsaved wound he is subject to Frenzy until the end of his next turn — not negated by being knocked down or stunned.

ABILITYINFO.name: Strange Allure
ABILITYINFO.text: An enemy wishing to attack her in melee or with a missile must first pass a Leadership test; on a failure it must re-roll any successful hits against her.

ABILITYINFO.name: Pound of Flesh
ABILITYINFO.text: A Wretch may be carried as a "meat shield": when the Merchant would take a wound, the Wretch is taken out of action instead. The Wretch still attacks in melee, has no base and cannot be targeted; it is lost if the Merchant dies.

ABILITYINFO.name: Cruel Fate
ABILITYINFO.text: Any captives the warband gains may be turned into Wretches at no cost (via lobotomy and torture).

ABILITYINFO.name: Flesh Reserve
ABILITYINFO.text: A Wretch may be carried as a "sacrificial pawn": when the Priest fails to cast a spell he may sacrifice it to re-roll the casting. The Wretch still attacks in melee, has no base and cannot be targeted; it is lost if the Priest dies.

ABILITYINFO.name: Fluctuating Form
ABILITYINFO.text: At the start of the battle and each Recovery phase (if not in combat) choose Male (+1 WS, S, T, A) or Female (+1 BS, +2 I, +2 Ld, +2 M).

ABILITYINFO.name: Slaves to Darkness
ABILITYINFO.text: Immune to all psychology and All Alone tests while within 6" of a friendly Hero. Wretches can never become Heroes; a repeated "The Lad's Got Talent" instead gives an immediate Serious Injury (as a Hero).

ABILITYINFO.name: Danse Macabre
ABILITYINFO.text: In the shooting phase target one enemy within 6" who must pass an Ld test or be controlled — moved anywhere (even out of combat or off a height). It tests Ld each recovery phase to break free and meanwhile cannot shoot, charge, cast or attack. Only one victim at a time.

ABILITYINFO.name: Expert Horseman
ABILITYINFO.text: The Aristocrat has the Ride (Nightmare) skill and may ride a Nightmare.

ABILITYINFO.name: Magical Adept
ABILITYINFO.text: When hired you may pay +10 gc to make the Scholar a Wizard; he then uses Lesser Magic and starts with one randomly generated spell.

ABILITYINFO.name: Story Teller
ABILITYINFO.text: If not upgraded to a Wizard, you may pay +10 gc to make the Scholar a Chronicler: re-roll one extra Exploration die and choose which two of the dice to keep.

ABILITYINFO.name: Maddened With Pain
ABILITYINFO.text: As soon as the Great Bear suffers a single wound it gains one additional Attack.

ABILITYINFO.name: Survivor
ABILITYINFO.text: Cannot be taken Out of Action unless already Knocked Down or Stunned (if standing, treat the result as Stunned instead). The first "Dead" rolled on the Serious Injury chart is treated as Multiple Injuries instead — once only.

ABILITYINFO.name: Ranger
ABILITYINFO.text: If the Guide is not put out of action, roll two dice for Exploration and pick one as the result (this is not a re-roll).

ABILITYINFO.name: Loner
ABILITYINFO.text: Immune to all All Alone tests, never claims or keeps a Gnoblar, and may never become the warband Leader.

ABILITYINFO.name: Feral Instinct
ABILITYINFO.text: At the start of your turn the Sabretusk must pass a Leadership test (use the Guide's Ld within 6"). If it fails, the opponent moves it this turn — it may even charge models from its own warband.

ABILITYINFO.name: Trained
ABILITYINFO.text: May use the Mountain Guide's Leadership within 6". With no Guide in the warband (death/injury) the Sabretusks must be caged at camp and cannot be used until a Guide is hired again.

ABILITYINFO.name: Gluttony
ABILITYINFO.text: Each Ogre counts as two models when selling wyrdstone or treasure. A captive (or any own member, animal or mount) may be devoured: reduce the warband's model count by 1 (2 for a Large captive) and an Ogre Hero gains XP equal to models eaten. Each Ogre always counts as at least one model.

ABILITYINFO.name: Lightning Reflexes
ABILITYINFO.text: When charged, the model strikes first; since chargers also strike first, resolve the order by comparing Initiative.

ABILITYINFO.name: Hireling
ABILITYINFO.text: Paid by the Merchant — this warrior may never become the warband Leader.

ABILITYINFO.name: Unreliable Hirelings
ABILITYINFO.text: May never become Heroes — re-roll any "The Lad's Got Talent" result.

ABILITYINFO.name: Strongman
ABILITYINFO.text: May use a double-handed weapon (or a similar "strike last" weapon) without the usual penalty of always striking last — work out the order of combat as normal.

ABILITYINFO.name: Wagon
ABILITYINFO.text: Follows the Wagon rules (Empire in Flames). One warband model must drive it. If destroyed, all stored equipment and treasures are lost; if abandoned after a failed Rout test with no driver, it falls to the enemy.

ABILITYINFO.name: Animosity
ABILITYINFO.text: At the start of each turn roll a D6 — on a 1 the warband squabbles and does nothing else that turn. Only Goblins are affected (not Trolls, Squigs, Snotlings or Fanatics).

ABILITYINFO.name: Hate Stunties
ABILITYINFO.text: Night Goblins are subject to Hatred towards Dwarfs. Fanatics are too far gone to be affected.

ABILITYINFO.name: Addict
ABILITYINFO.text: Must buy Mad Cap Mushrooms before each game; if none are available he stays in his cave and does not take part.

ABILITYINFO.name: Mushroom Brain
ABILITYINFO.text: Prolonged Mad Cap use has fried his brain — he cannot gain Experience.

ABILITYINFO.name: Looney
ABILITYINFO.text: Subject to Frenzy, and must test for permanent damage after the battle (as for the Ball and Chain).

ABILITYINFO.name: Frantic
ABILITYINFO.text: Hyperactive — always strikes first in combat, ignoring weapon and Initiative modifiers.

ABILITYINFO.name: Minderz
ABILITYINFO.text: Must stay within 6" of a Goblin or it goes wild: from then on it moves 2D6" in a random direction each Movement phase, out of the player's control until the end of the game.

ABILITYINFO.name: Weedy
ABILITYINFO.text: Fragile — when wounded, knocked down on a 1, stunned on 2-3, out of action on 4-6.

ABILITYINFO.name: Dodgy
ABILITYINFO.text: Constantly ducking and diving — a 6+ dodge save against shooting.

ABILITYINFO.name: Insignificant
ABILITYINFO.text: The whole mob counts as a single model for Rout tests, maximum warband size and income. An enemy only gains Experience for taking the last Snotling out of action.

ABILITYINFO.name: Duellist
ABILITYINFO.text: When fighting a single opponent in melee, the Saurus Slayer may re-roll missed hits on the first turn of that combat, whether he charged or was charged.

ABILITYINFO.name: Silent Hunter
ABILITYINFO.text: Always counts as Hidden while touching any terrain ≥1" high. Shooting does not reveal his position (casting a spell does).

ABILITYINFO.name: Surprise Attack
ABILITYINFO.text: May re-roll missed to-hit rolls against an enemy attacked from its rear (a 180° arc behind its facing).

ABILITYINFO.name: Canopy Walker
ABILITYINFO.text: Automatically passes all climbing, jumping and falling-off-ledges tests.

ABILITYINFO.name: Deadeye
ABILITYINFO.text: Suffers no to-hit penalty for shooting at long range.

ABILITYINFO.name: Traps
ABILITYINFO.text: Starts with 1 Trap; may buy up to 5 more at 5 gc each (one use only). Used in the shooting phase: place a real and a false 1" counter within 3" of the Trapmaster (≥3" from other models). A model moving within 2" flips one — a real trap deals D3 Strength 5 hits (no criticals).

ABILITYINFO.name: War Beasts
ABILITYINFO.text: With a Beastmaster you may include up to 2 War Beasts (Warhound, Barbary Monkey, Hunting Hawk). They count toward the maximum warband size, must be bought at creation, and may use the Beastmaster's Leadership within 6".

ABILITYINFO.name: Promotion Only
ABILITYINFO.text: Re-roll any "The Lad's Got Talent" result. A Prospect can only become a Hero via the Promotions rule (replacing a lost Hero); you may then hire a new Prospect.

ABILITYINFO.name: Nimble
ABILITYINFO.text: Climbs any sheer surface without Initiative tests, re-rolls failed Diving Charge tests, and may enter buildings. Has a 5+ ward save against any wound (melee, shooting, magic or falling).

ABILITYINFO.name: Expert Riggers
ABILITYINFO.text: Boatswains move through rigging, masts and ropes without movement penalties and ignore climbing penalties.

ABILITYINFO.name: Rabble
ABILITYINFO.text: Lowly Swabbies: gain no experience and never count toward the minimum size of the warband.

ABILITYINFO.name: Stream of Corruption
ABILITYINFO.text: Shooting attack, range 6", Strength 3, no armour saves allowed.

ABILITYINFO.name: Nurgle's Rot
ABILITYINFO.text: Immune to all poisons. On an unmodified to-wound roll of 6 in close combat, the target contracts Nurgle's Rot (living models only). Before each battle the infected model tests Toughness or permanently loses 1 Toughness (dies at 0); rolling a 6 also passes the Rot to another random warband member.

ABILITYINFO.name: Cloud of Flies
ABILITYINFO.text: A swarm of flies buzzes around the bearer and his close-combat opponent. The opponent suffers a -1 to hit modifier on all attacks.

ABILITYINFO.name: Bloated Foulness
ABILITYINFO.text: A huge, disgusting mass of diseased flesh: +1 Wound and +1 Toughness, but Movement is reduced by -1.

ABILITYINFO.name: Mark of Nurgle
ABILITYINFO.text: Branded with Nurgle's three spheres: +1 Wound and immune to all poisons.

ABILITYINFO.name: Hideous
ABILITYINFO.text: So disgusting to behold that the model causes fear.

ABILITYINFO.name: Daemon Soul
ABILITYINFO.text: A Daemon lives within the mutant's soul, granting a 4+ save against the effects of spells or prayers.

ABILITYINFO.name: Great Claw
ABILITYINFO.text: One arm ends in a great crab-like claw: may carry no weapon in that arm, but gains an extra close-combat attack at +1 Strength.

ABILITYINFO.name: Cloven Hooves
ABILITYINFO.text: +1 Movement.

ABILITYINFO.name: Tentacles
ABILITYINFO.text: One arm ends in a tentacle. May grapple the close-combat opponent to reduce his Attacks by -1 (minimum 1); the mutant chooses which attack is lost.

ABILITYINFO.name: Black Blood
ABILITYINFO.text: Whenever the model loses a Wound in close combat, anyone in base contact suffers a Strength 3 hit (no critical hits) from the corrosive, spurting blood.

ABILITYINFO.name: Spines
ABILITYINFO.text: Any model in base contact suffers an automatic Strength 1 hit at the start of each close-combat phase. Spines never cause critical hits.

ABILITYINFO.name: Scorpion Tail
ABILITYINFO.text: A barbed, venomed tail grants an extra Strength 5 attack each close-combat phase (Strength 2 against models immune to poison).

ABILITYINFO.name: Extra Arm
ABILITYINFO.text: May wield a single-handed weapon (or a shield/buckler) in the extra arm for +1 Attack in close combat.
